mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in retail sales volume indicates synchronized demand-supply mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active federal funds rate hikes, a month-over-month increase in core inflation readings accelerates real rate expectations, forcing equity revaluation downward and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero when the trigger activates, or the interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in new housing starts reflects robust consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically correlates with positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between HOUST month-over-month changes and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 105.0, it indicates manufacturing sector weakness that precedes broader economic slowdowns, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following the threshold breach, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in walk-forward analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment growth exceeds 200,000 while retail sales volume declines signals wage-driven inflation without corresponding demand, prompting Fed hawkishness and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient (p > 0.10) in a panel regression predicting 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate environment where the federal funds rate stays below 1.0%, a sharp quarterly contraction in real GDP growth triggers liquidity-driven equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or statistically neutral when real GDP contracts under the specified rate threshold, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 5-year window.
